title: Method for determining whether a subject is at risk of developing a chronic respiratory disease
abstract: A method for determining whether a subject is at risk of developing a chronic respiratory disease or chronic respiratory condition is disclosed.
claims:
Claims 57 CLAIMS 1 . A method for determining whether a subj ect is at ris k of developing a chronic respiratory disease or chronic respiratory condition ; wherein the method comprises determining in a biological sample obtained from the subj ect a quantitative value of a plurality of biomarkers comprising at least three biomarkers of the following in the biological sample : - ratio of monounsaturated fatty acids and/or of oleic acid to total fatty acids , - glycoprotein acetyls , - albumin and/or total protein, - ratio of docosahexaenoic acid to total fatty acids , - ratio of linoleic acid to total fatty acids - ratio of omega-3 fatty acids to total fatty acids , - ratio of omega- 6 fatty acids to total fatty acids , - fatty acid degree of unsaturation, - docosahexaenoic acid, - linoleic acid, monounsaturated fatty acids and/or oleic acid, - omega-3 fatty acids , - omega- 6 fatty acids , - high-density lipoprotein (HDL) triglycerides , - low-density lipoprotein (LDL) triglycerides , - acetate , - lactate , - pyruvate , - glutamine , - histidine , - phenylalanine ; and 

 58 comparing the quantitative value (s) of the plurality of the biomarkers to a control sample or to a control value; wherein an increase or a decrease in the quantitative value (s) of the plurality of the biomarkers, when compared to the control sample or to the control value, is/are indicative of the subject having an increased risk of developing the chronic respiratory disease or condition; and wherein the quantitative value of the plurality of the biomarkers is/are measured using nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the method comprises determining in the biological sample quantitative values of the plurality of biomarkers, wherein the plurality of the biomarkers comprises four, five or more biomarkers. 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the plurality of the biomarkers comprises or is the ratio of monounsaturated fatty acids and/or of oleic acid to total fatty acids. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 3, wherein the plurality of the biomarkers comprises or is glycoprotein acetyls. 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 4, wherein the method comprises determining in the biological sample obtained from the subject a quantitative value of the following biomarkers: - glycoprotein acetyls; - albumin and/or total protein; and comparing the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers to a control sample or to a control value (s) ; wherein an increase or a decrease in the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers, when compared to the control sample or to the control value, is/are indicative of the subject having an increased risk of developing the chronic respiratory disease or condition. 

 59 6. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 5, wherein the method comprises determining in the biological sample obtained from the subject a quantitative value of the following biomarkers: - glycoprotein acetyls, - at least one fatty acid measure (s) of the following: ratio of docosahexaenoic acid to total fatty acids, docosahexaenoic acid, ratio of linoleic acid to total fatty acids, linoleic acid, ratio of monounsaturated fatty acids and/or of oleic acid to total fatty acids, monounsaturated fatty acids and/or oleic acid, ratio of omega-3 fatty acids to total fatty acids, omega-3 fatty acids, ratio of omega-6 fatty acids to total fatty acids, omega-6 fatty acids, fatty acid degree of unsaturation; and comparing the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers to a control sample or to a control value (s) ; wherein an increase or a decrease in the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers, when compared to the control sample or to the control value, is/are indicative of the subject having an increased risk of developing the chronic respiratory disease or condition. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 6, wherein the chronic respiratory disease comprises or is a malignant neoplasm of respiratory or intrathoracic organs (C30-C39) ; chronic lower respiratory disease (J40-J47) ; lung disease due to an external agent (J60-J70) ; interstitial pulmonary disease (J80-J84) ; other disease of the pleura (J90- J94) ; or other disease of the respiratory system (J96- J99) . 8. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 7, wherein the chronic respiratory disease comprises or is lung cancer (C34) ; bronchitis (J40) ; emphysema (J43) ; chronic obstructive respiratory disease (J44) ; asthma (J45) ; bronchiectasis (J47) ; pneumonitis (J69) ; pulmonary edema (J81) ; other interstitial pulmonary 

 60 diseases (J84) ; pleural effusion (J90) ; pleural plaque (J92) ; pneumothorax and air leak (J93) ; respiratory failure (J96) ; or other respiratory disorder (J98) . 9. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 8, wherein the chronic respiratory disease comprises or is lung cancer, chronic obstructive respiratory disease (COPD) , asthma, emphysema, bronchiectasis, pneumonitis, interstitial pulmonary diseases, pleural effusion, and/or respiratory failure. 10. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 9, wherein the method comprises determining in the biological sample obtained from the subject a quantitative value of the following biomarkers: - ratio of monounsaturated fatty acids and/or of oleic acid to total fatty acids, - glycoprotein acetyls, - albumin and/or total protein, - ratio of docosahexaenoic acid to total fatty acids , - ratio of omega-3 fatty acids to total fatty acids , ratio of omega-6 fatty acids and/or of linoleic acid to total fatty acids, - fatty acid degree of unsaturation, - docosahexaenoic acid, monounsaturated fatty acids and/or oleic acid, - omega-3 fatty acids, - omega-6 fatty acids and/or linoleic acid, - glutamine, - histidine, - phenylalanine, - high-density lipoprotein (HDL triglycerides) , - low-density lipoprotein (LDL) triglycerides, - acetate, - lactate, 

 61 - pyruvate; and comparing the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers to a control sample or to a control value (s) ; wherein an increase or a decrease in the quantitative value (s) of the biomarkers, when compared to the control sample or to the control value, is/are indicative of the subject having an increased risk of developing the chronic respiratory disease or condition. 11. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 10, wherein the method further comprises determining whether the subject is at risk of developing the chronic respiratory disease or chronic respiratory condition using a risk score, hazard ratio, odds ratio, and/or predicted absolute risk or relative risk calculated on the basis of the quantitative value (s) of the plurality of the biomarkers. 12. The method according to any one of claim 11, wherein the risk score, hazard ratio, odds ratio, and/or predicted relative risk and/or absolute risk is calculated on the basis of at least one further measure, such as a characteristic of the subject. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the characteristic of the subject is one or more of age, height, weight, body mass index, race or ethnic group, smoking, and/or family history of chronic respiratory diseases . 14. The method according to any one of claims 1 - 13, wherein the subject is a healthy human.
